new_classes.label_en: PDO wine production process (CN 2204 29 38, containers > 10 l)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine production process for wine with a protected designation of origin (PDO), presented in containers holding more than 10 litres, classified under CN 2204 29 38.

new_classes.label_en: PDO wine in containers > 10 l — other (CN 2204 29 38)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes with a protected designation of origin (PDO) as defined in additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers holding more than 10 litres, classified under CN 2204 29 38. This is the residual 'other' subheading within the PDO wine large-container group, distinct from CN 2204 29 28.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three main discriminating criteria for CN 2204 29 38: (1) PDO wine quality (has quality PDOWineQuality, grounded in additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22), (2) container size > 10 l (has part LargeContainerOver10L), and (3) residual 'other' character within the large-container PDO group (complement of PDOWineLargeContainer22042928, which is CN 2204 29 28). The production process is linked to the PDO wine region site. Coverage is not 1.0 because: (a) the internal subdivision criterion that distinguishes 22042928 from 22042938 (e.g. specific colour — white vs. other, or specific named PDO appellations) is not fully elaborated in the provided legal note (the note only states 'PDO wine; see additional note 6(a)'); (b) additional note 6(a) itself is referenced but not reproduced in the supplied legal text, so any further sub-distinctions within the large-container PDO group (beyond complement of 22042928) cannot be encoded with certainty. The complement restriction on 22042928 serves as a residual catch-all within the sibling group.
